vBRIEFInfo.description: Cleanup of the 3 non-blocking P2s flagged on #1378 Story C (PR #1402).

plan.title: chore(scripts): address #1378 Story C P2 follow-ups in preflight_story_start.py

narratives.Description: Fold the three non-blocking P2 review findings from #1378 Story C (PR #1402) into scripts/preflight_story_start.py: (1) remove the redundant double parse_allocation_section call between evaluate() and main(); (2) broaden the _git_porcelain spawn-failure catch from FileNotFoundError to OSError so PermissionError/NotADirectoryError fail closed (config error) instead of crashing; (3) correct the OK-message wording so a --allow-dirty run does not claim 'tree clean'.
narratives.UserStory: As a maintainer, I want the story-start Gate 0 script free of the three P2 nits so the gate's diagnostics are accurate and it fails closed on any git spawn failure.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1. evaluate() gains an optional pre-parsed `parsed` kwarg (defaults to None => parse internally, preserving the 2-tuple return + existing tests); main() parses once and passes it, deriving dispatch_kind from the same parse. 2. _git_porcelain catches OSError (not just FileNotFoundError). 3. evaluate() computes a tree-state note (tree clean | dirty tree allowed (--allow-dirty)) and uses it in all three OK messages. 4. Add tests: --allow-dirty OK message wording, and _git_porcelain returns None on a PermissionError spawn failure. 5. CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry referencing #1378.
narratives.Traces: Traces to GitHub issue #1378 Story C P2 follow-ups (PR #1402 review).

narrative.Acceptance: main() parses the allocation section exactly once and passes the result to evaluate() via an optional `parsed` kwarg; evaluate()'s (exit_code, message) return arity is unchanged and all existing tests pass.
narrative.Traces: Traces to #1378 Story C P2 -- double parse.

narrative.Acceptance: _git_porcelain returns None (=> config error exit 2) on any OSError spawning git (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, NotADirectoryError), proven by a test that monkeypatches subprocess.run to raise PermissionError.
narrative.Traces: Traces to #1378 Story C P2 -- OSError catch.

narrative.Acceptance: When --allow-dirty lets a dirty tree through, the OK message reports 'dirty tree allowed (--allow-dirty)' rather than 'tree clean'; a clean tree still reports 'tree clean'. A test asserts the dirty-allowed wording.
narrative.Traces: Traces to #1378 Story C P2 -- message wording.

references.title: Issue #1378: first-class allocation-plan reference in dispatch envelope (post #1371 deterministic-tier enabler)
